heading: (intro)
raw: Directed and written by Richard Brooks, based on the book by Truman Capote.
clean: Directed and written by Richard Brooks, based on the book by Truman Capote.

heading: Dick Hickock
raw: Doctors. They made a dwarf out of me. Doctors and lawyers. What do they care? Ever see a millionaire fry in the electric chair? Hell, no. There's two kinds of laws, honey: One for the rich and one for the poor.
clean: Doctors. They made a dwarf out of me. Doctors and lawyers. What do they care? Ever see a millionaire fry in the electric chair? Hell, no. There's two kinds of laws, honey: One for the rich and one for the poor.

heading: Perry Smith
raw: It doesn't make sense. I mean what happened. It had nothing to do with the Clutters. They never hurt me. They just happened to be there. I thought Mr. Clutter was a very nice gentleman... I thought so right up to the time I cut his throat.
clean: It doesn't make sense. I mean what happened. It had nothing to do with the Clutters. They never hurt me. They just happened to be there. I thought Mr. Clutter was a very nice gentleman... I thought so right up to the time I cut his throat.

heading: Perry Smith
raw: I despise people who can't control themselves.
clean: I despise people who can't control themselves.

heading: Perry Smith
raw: I'd like to apologize, but... who to?
clean: I'd like to apologize, but... who to?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Nancy Clutter: Daddy, you know Jolene? I promised to teach her how to bake a cherry pie.
clean: Daddy, you know Jolene? I promised to teach her how to bake a cherry pie.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Herb Clutter: Sounds serious.
clean: Sounds serious.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Nancy Clutter: She insists on today.
clean: She insists on today.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Herb Clutter: That makes it practically a catastrophe.
clean: That makes it practically a catastrophe.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: It's true! Really true! We're on our way and never coming back. Never! And no regrets.
clean: It's true! Really true! We're on our way and never coming back. Never! And no regrets.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: For you. You're leaving nothing. What about my old man... and my mother? They'll still be there when my checks start bouncing.
clean: For you. You're leaving nothing. What about my old man... and my mother? They'll still be there when my checks start bouncing.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: It's nice the way you think about your folks.
clean: It's nice the way you think about your folks.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: Yeah! I'm a real thoughtful bastard.
clean: Yeah! I'm a real thoughtful bastard.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: That was stupid - stealin' a lousy pack of razor blades! To prove what?
clean: That was stupid - stealin' a lousy pack of razor blades! To prove what?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: It's the national pastime, baby, stealin' and cheatin'. If they ever count every cheatin' wife and tax chiseler, the whole country would be behind prison walls.
clean: It's the national pastime, baby, stealin' and cheatin'. If they ever count every cheatin' wife and tax chiseler, the whole country would be behind prison walls.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: Where are the black stockings?
clean: Where are the black stockings?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: They didn't have black.
clean: They didn't have black.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: Stop someplace else.
clean: Stop someplace else.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: Where?
clean: Where?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: A Catholic hospital.
clean: A Catholic hospital.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: Sure. We'll just barge in like it was a goddamn five-and-dime.
clean: Sure. We'll just barge in like it was a goddamn five-and-dime.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: Sister, sweetie, we gotta have some black stockings to hide our face.
clean: Sister, sweetie, we gotta have some black stockings to hide our face.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: Okay, forget it. Anyway, nuns are bad luck.
clean: Okay, forget it. Anyway, nuns are bad luck.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: Kind of sexy, though.
clean: Kind of sexy, though.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: You wouldn't think so if you'd lived in one of those orphan homes... with those black widows always at you. Always sneaking up in the dark, spying while you sleep. Hitting you with a flashlight for wetting the bed. That's the first time I was saved by the yellow bird.
clean: You wouldn't think so if you'd lived in one of those orphan homes... with those black widows always at you. Always sneaking up in the dark, spying while you sleep. Hitting you with a flashlight for wetting the bed. That's the first time I was saved by the yellow bird.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: The what?
clean: The what?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: Sort of a parrot. Taller than Jesus. Brilliant yellow, like a sunflower. It attacked those nuns like an avenging angel. The nuns begged for mercy. But the yellow bird slaughtered them anyway. Then the bird folded me in its wings...and lifted me up to paradise.
clean: Sort of a parrot. Taller than Jesus. Brilliant yellow, like a sunflower. It attacked those nuns like an avenging angel. The nuns begged for mercy. But the yellow bird slaughtered them anyway. Then the bird folded me in its wings...and lifted me up to paradise.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: That's one hell of a bird to have on your side.
clean: That's one hell of a bird to have on your side.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: Anyway, that's why I have an aversion to nuns...and God and religion.
clean: Anyway, that's why I have an aversion to nuns...and God and religion.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: Forget it. Black stockings are a waste of time. No one's gonna remember us because we're leaving no witnesses.
clean: Forget it. Black stockings are a waste of time. No one's gonna remember us because we're leaving no witnesses.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: Next move... Mexico. Once we beat it out of the country.
clean: Next move... Mexico. Once we beat it out of the country.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: On what? $43 and a smile and bullshit.
clean: On what? $43 and a smile and bullshit.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: You guessed it, chief. It's the smile that does it. Like it says in the commercials, the family that sticks together lives forever.
clean: You guessed it, chief. It's the smile that does it. Like it says in the commercials, the family that sticks together lives forever.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reporter: They say Nancy's boyfriend is the prime suspect.
clean: They say Nancy's boyfriend is the prime suspect.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Alvin Dewey: Is that what they say?
clean: Is that what they say?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reporter: Well, he was the last one to see them alive.
clean: Well, he was the last one to see them alive.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Alvin Dewey: Except for the killers.
clean: Except for the killers.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reporter: What about Perry? Don't you get along?
clean: What about Perry? Don't you get along?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: Nobody can get along with him. There's five guys waiting here for the big swing. Little Perry's the only one yapping against capital punishment.
clean: Nobody can get along with him. There's five guys waiting here for the big swing. Little Perry's the only one yapping against capital punishment.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reporter: Don't tell me you're for it?
clean: Don't tell me you're for it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Dick: Hell, hanging's only getting revenge. What's wrong with revenge? I've been revenging myself all my life. Sure. I'm for hanging. Just so long as I'm not the one being hanged.
clean: Hell, hanging's only getting revenge. What's wrong with revenge? I've been revenging myself all my life. Sure. I'm for hanging. Just so long as I'm not the one being hanged.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Perry: When you hit the end of the rope... your muscles lose control. I'm afraid I'll mess myself.
clean: When you hit the end of the rope... your muscles lose control. I'm afraid I'll mess myself.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Prison Guard: It's nothing to be ashamed of. They all do it.
clean: It's nothing to be ashamed of. They all do it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Alvin Dewey: Coming?
clean: Coming?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reporter: What for? What does it add up to, anyway?
clean: What for? What does it add up to, anyway?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Reporter: Well, maybe this will help to stop it.
clean: Well, maybe this will help to stop it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Alvin Dewey: Never has.
clean: Never has.
